title: Aureline artifact-class and packet-class registry row
description: Boundary schema for the artifact-class rows seeded in artifacts/governance/packet_class_registry.yaml and explained in docs/governance/artifact_hierarchy_and_packet_classes.md. An artifact_class_row freezes one of the canonical control-artifact families (requirement register and alias crosswalk, ADR/RFC, design packet, contract/schema/interface packet, verification corpus and benchmark plan, compatibility or certified-archetype report, claim manifest and docs/migration pack, runbook/support/shiproom packet, public-proof publication bundle) so reviewers can tell which packets, reports, and registries are authoritative, how they link to one another, and when each class is release-bearing. The class row carries owner lane, canonical id namespace, source-of-truth location, visibility class, freshness class, minimum backlinks across the artifact graph, release uses, and a release-use matrix that names presence and missing-or-stale effect at each governed milestone. Adding a release-use value, presence value, freshness value, or backlink-class value is additive-minor and bumps artifact_class_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and opens a decision row.

artifact_class_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this artifact-class vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

path_ref.description: Repo-relative path or document reference. Consumers treat it as opaque text.

posture_note.description: Short, reviewable sentence that clarifies a class-level posture.

artifact_class_id_value.description: Closed vocabulary of canonical artifact classes covered by the registry. Adding a class is additive-minor and bumps artifact_class_schema_version.

owner_lane_value.description: Owner lane drawn from artifacts/governance/ownership_matrix.yaml scorecard_lane_index. Values outside this list MUST first land a new row in the ownership matrix.

visibility_class.description: Whether the artifact class is a repository-internal control record (internal) or part of the project's public truth (public).

freshness_class.description: How often instances of the class are re-consulted. each_change is the strictest cadence; per_event covers incident, advisory, or partner-handoff packets that fire on a real-world trigger rather than a calendar.

release_use_value.description: Closed set of release uses an artifact class participates in. milestone_promotion gates milestone-close decisions; release_promotion gates release readiness; migration_or_compatibility carries cross-version skew or import/export truth; support_handoff carries field-runbook or escalation truth; audit_or_governance carries decision-rights or waiver-coverage truth; claim_publication binds public statements to evidence; public_proof carries reviewer-facing reproducibility packets; incident_or_emergency_response carries advisories, freezes, kill switches, or revocations.

presence_class.description: Whether instances of the class MUST exist at the named milestone. required_if_class_used is reserved for classes whose existence is conditional on a feature actually shipping (for example, a compatibility report when no public surface change lands). retired is reserved for milestones at which the class has been deprecated in favor of another class row.

milestone_slug.description: Canonical milestone slug. Values mirror schemas/governance/requirement_register.schema.json and MUST NOT be replaced with planning shorthand.

missing_or_stale_outcome.description: What happens when a required artifact class is missing or its instance is stale. no_effect is reserved for milestones at which the class is not_required or retired.

id_grammar.description: Plain-language description of the id grammar, including character set, separator, and any required prefix.

examples.description: Two or three reviewer-facing examples drawn from existing seeded rows.

canonical_locations.description: Repo-relative directories or files where instances live. Multiple entries are allowed when one class is intentionally split across docs/, artifacts/, and fixtures/ trees.

overview_doc_ref.description: Single human-readable narrative or contract document for the class.

schema_refs.description: Optional boundary schemas that constrain instances of the class.

freshness_policy_refs.description: Optional refs into the evidence freshness policy or SLO matrix that govern stale-after windows for the class.

rerun_trigger_refs.description: Optional refs into the evidence rerun-trigger catalog rows that invalidate instances before the nominal stale-after window expires.

required_backlink_classes.description: Other artifact classes that an instance MUST cite by canonical id before it is reviewable. Forms the directed back-edge graph that prevents parallel identities.

optional_backlink_classes.description: Classes that an instance MAY cite when the linked surface exists. Optional links never substitute for required backlinks.

release_use_matrix.description: One entry per governed milestone slug. The same milestone_slug MUST NOT appear twice in one row.

control_artifact_index_row_ref.description: id of the row in artifacts/governance/control_artifact_index.yaml that names the canonical home for the class. Every artifact-class row MUST resolve to exactly one control-artifact-index row.

source_anchor_map_class_ref.description: artifact_classes[].class_id from artifacts/governance/source_anchor_map.yaml that the class is grouped under for the source-anchor linter.

ownership_matrix_lane_ref.description: scorecard_lane_index entry that owns the class. Mirrors owner_lane and exists so source_anchor_map / ownership_matrix consumers can join without re-reading the row body.

related_class_ids.description: Other classes whose instances commonly compose with this class but are not strictly required as backlinks.

related_packet_template_refs.description: Optional packet-template documents that consumers reuse when authoring instances of the class.

title.description: Short human-readable title for the class.

description.description: One-paragraph statement of what the class governs and what it does not.

release_uses.description: Closed list of release uses the class participates in.

notes.description: Optional additional note for future consumers.
